project.name: Anyplan
project.purpose: Develop a portable guidance framework for AI-assisted software development and a visual interaction engine for browsing, editing, and adjusting instantiated project guidance.
project.context: The project is in its bootstrap stage. The current priority is to define a portable framework, instantiate it for Anyplan, derive generalized rules from the sibling Anygine project, and keep the visual engine aligned with the structured guidance contract.

principles.title: English documentation
principles.statement: Durable project documentation, guidance instances, ADRs, logs, framework specifications, and documentation-facing UI text should be written in English.
principles.rationale: A single documentation language keeps project memory searchable, reviewable, and reusable across future AI sessions and project instances.

principles.title: Portable framework first
principles.statement: The framework body must stay independent of Anyplan and Anygine implementation details; project-specific rules belong in project instances.
principles.rationale: Separating framework and instance allows the visual engine and guidance model to support many projects.

principles.title: Anygine-derived generalization
principles.statement: Framework rules should abstract the collaboration and governance patterns proven in Anygine without copying graphics-engine-specific details into the portable body.
principles.rationale: Anygine provides a concrete, working example, while Anyplan must remain usable by projects in other domains.

principles.title: Structure before visualization
principles.statement: The visual engine should read structured guidance data rather than infer workflows from prose documents.
principles.rationale: A stable data contract is easier to validate, version, and connect to tooling.

principles.title: Central working contract
principles.statement: Each project instance should identify a central guidance artifact that outranks descriptive documents when future work needs durable rules.
principles.rationale: Anygine shows that AI collaboration works better when rules, authority, and conflicts are anchored in one contract.

principles.title: Separate memory types
principles.statement: Rules, current state, decisions, evidence, roadmap state, development history, and methodology should be represented as distinct document types or records.
principles.rationale: Separating memory types helps AI and humans recover the right source of truth without rereading the whole archive.

principles.title: Vertical slice growth
principles.statement: Projects should prefer small runnable slices that validate architecture before large speculative frameworks.
principles.rationale: Anygine's roadmap demonstrates that implementation pressure is the best test of module boundaries and workflow assumptions.

principles.title: Self application
principles.statement: Anyplan development must record task intent, workflow stages, validation results, changed guidance, and open questions.
principles.rationale: The framework should expose its own gaps through practical use.

roles.name: Human Project Owner
responsibilities: Define research direction, product intent, and acceptance criteria
Decide whether core framework concepts are valid
Approve high-impact changes to framework authority, schema, or project direction
permissions: Accept, reject, or redirect AI proposals
handoffRules: AI may implement a scoped minimum version when ambiguity is low-risk
Human confirmation is required when a change redefines framework authority, schema semantics, or core project direction

roles.name: AI Coding Agent
responsibilities: Recover project context from the guidance map and repository state
Maintain framework documents, schema, project instances, and the visual engine
Check new requests against durable guidance
Implement scoped code and documentation changes
Run available validation and report residual risk
permissions: Propose workflow, constraint, interface, and document-map changes
handoffRules: Surface material conflicts before executing contradictory work
Return to the human owner for destructive operations or non-inferable product decisions

roles.name: Visual Interaction Engine
responsibilities: Display documents, workflows, constraints, and interfaces
Allow users to edit project instance guidance
Export guidance JSON or future guidance patches
permissions: Create updated JSON after user interaction
handoffRules: Framework-body changes should become explicit proposals rather than silent writes
Exported JSON should be checked by schema validation or human review

workflows.name: Default AI Collaboration Loop
entrypoints: A human asks for implementation, review, research, or documentation work
AI detects a gap in the framework, project instance, or visual engine
The visual engine exports a proposed workflow or guidance adjustment

steps.title: Capture Intent
steps.purpose: Turn a natural-language request into an executable task boundary.
actions: Identify goal, scope, acceptance clues, and unknowns
Make reasonable assumptions only when risk is low
checks: High-impact choices are flagged for human confirmation

steps.title: Recover Context
steps.purpose: Read the smallest authoritative context needed to act safely.
actions: Read central guidance, current state, relevant documents, and current files
Identify existing user changes and relevant source-of-truth documents
Summarize the current structure and risks
checks: The selected context is sufficient for the task

steps.title: Check Guidance
steps.purpose: Compare the request against durable project rules and source-of-truth documents.
actions: Identify conflicts with language policy, authority, schema, workflow, or project boundaries
Raise conflicts or propose document updates when direction intentionally changes
checks: Intentional direction changes are recorded in the appropriate document

steps.title: Plan Change
steps.purpose: Convert the task into scoped, verifiable implementation steps.
actions: Update the task plan when the work is substantial
checks: The plan is proportionate to the task
The plan preserves framework and instance boundaries

steps.title: Implement Change
actions: Edit documents, schema, project instances, or engine code
Keep domain-specific rules in instances and general rules in the framework
checks: The structured data contract remains valid

steps.title: Verify Change
steps.purpose: Check that the change is valid, useful, and human-reproducible.
actions: Run JSON, schema, syntax, or runtime checks as available
Report what was and was not verified

steps.title: Record Decision
steps.purpose: Preserve useful project memory without unnecessary ceremony.
actions: Update the smallest authoritative document set
Create or update ADRs when guidance or architecture changes
Record open questions and next useful actions
checks: Completed work and remaining risk are clear
Future sessions can recover the reason for durable changes

exitCriteria: The requested artifact has been changed or the blocker has been explained
Durable guidance changes are recorded in the appropriate document

constraints.rule: Durable project documentation and guidance text must be written in English.
constraints.validation: Search project documentation and guidance files for non-English durable prose before completion.

constraints.rule: Do not revert or overwrite user changes that were not created in the current task.
constraints.validation: Check git status before and after edits; work with existing changes instead of reverting them.

constraints.rule: The visual engine and project instances must treat the schema and guidance JSON as the structured contract.
constraints.validation: Guidance JSON must parse and validate against the schema whenever schema validation is available.

constraints.rule: Portable rules belong in the framework; project-specific rules belong in project instances.
constraints.validation: Framework documents should describe abstract rules; instances should bind them to concrete project choices.

constraints.rule: Rules derived from Anygine must be expressed as generalized collaboration, boundary, validation, or documentation patterns.
constraints.validation: Framework text should explain how Anygine can instantiate the rule without making every project inherit Vulkan, C++, CMake, or Conan.

constraints.rule: Each serious project instance should identify a central guidance artifact and a document authority model.
constraints.validation: The document map should identify the primary contract, decision records, current state, evidence, roadmap, and logs when they exist.

constraints.rule: Technical evidence and binding decisions should be recorded separately.
constraints.validation: Research reports may recommend actions, but durable decisions should appear in a decision log or ADR.

constraints.rule: Prefer a small runnable or inspectable slice before expanding a large abstraction.
constraints.validation: Plans should define the smallest useful verification path before broad architecture expansion.

constraints.rule: Completion summaries should include validation that the project owner can reproduce when relevant.
constraints.validation: Final reports should include commands, expected results, and unverified items for meaningful changes.

constraints.rule: The visual engine may edit project instances; framework-body changes require explicit change records.
constraints.validation: Changes under framework/ should update the collaboration log and should add an ADR when they alter durable direction.

interfaces.name: TaskBrief
interfaces.purpose: Convert a user request into an executable task object.

fields.name: intent
fields.description: The core outcome requested by the user.

fields.name: scope
fields.description: Files, modules, concepts, or documents allowed in the task.

fields.name: acceptance
fields.description: Signals that the task is complete.

fields.name: unknowns
fields.description: Questions that remain uncertain but can be recorded or clarified.

interfaces.name: ContextSnapshot
interfaces.purpose: Record the project context recovered by the AI collaborator.

fields.name: filesRead
fields.description: Important files read during the task.

fields.name: currentStructure
fields.description: Summary of the current repository or guidance structure.

fields.name: risks
fields.description: Risks that may affect implementation or validation.

interfaces.name: GuidanceConflict
interfaces.purpose: Represent a possible conflict between a new request and existing durable guidance.

fields.name: request
fields.description: The request or change that may conflict with existing guidance.

fields.name: conflictingGuidance
fields.description: Guidance documents, principles, or constraints that appear to conflict.

interfaces.name: WorkflowPatch
interfaces.purpose: Describe an adjustment to workflow nodes, order, or checks.

interfaces.name: GuidanceChange
interfaces.purpose: Describe a proposed change to framework or project guidance.

fields.name: changeClass
fields.description: Scope of the proposed guidance change.

fields.name: affectedDocuments
fields.description: Documents or structured files affected by the change.

fields.name: migrationNotes
fields.description: Migration guidance when existing instances or tools are affected.

interfaces.name: VerificationReport
interfaces.purpose: Record validation commands, results, and residual risk.

fields.name: checks
fields.description: Each validation command or method and its result.

fields.name: humanReproduction
fields.description: Steps the project owner can run independently.

documents.title: ADR 0002: English Docs and Anygine-Derived Framework

changeControl.versioning: During the 0.x stage, the schema may evolve quickly. Removing a field or changing field semantics requires a minor version bump and migration notes.
proposalRequiredFor: Removing or renaming a top-level schema field
Changing the meaning of the default workflow stages
Letting the visual engine write directly to portable framework documents
Introducing runtime dependencies that require external services or authentication
reviewChecklist: Does the change keep the framework independent of concrete project details?
Does the Anyplan instance need a synchronized update?
Can the visual engine read or safely ignore the new field?
Does the change preserve the Anygine-derived abstraction without hard-coding Anygine domain rules?
Does the collaboration log or ADR record the motivation, validation, and residual risk?
